heading: Energy resources of Caribbean and Pacific insular areas
label: 1492 Energy resources of Caribbean and Pacific insular areas

children.text: the Caribbean and Pacific insular areas of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, the Marshall Islands, and Palau are virtually completely dependent on imported sources of energy;

the dependence of such areas on imported sources of energy coupled with the increasing cost and the uncertain availability and supply of such sources of energy will continue to frustrate the political, social, and economic development of such areas by placing increasingly severe fiscal burdens on the local governments of these areas;

these insular areas are endowed with a variety of renewable sources of energy which, if developed, would alleviate their dependence on imported sources of energy, relieve the fiscal burden on local governments imposed by the costs of imported fuel, and strengthen the base for political, social, and economic development;

appropriate technologies are presently available to develop the renewable energy resources of these insular areas but that comprehensive energy plans have not been adequately developed to meet the energy demands of these areas from renewable energy resources;

electric power transmission and distribution lines in insular areas are inadequate to withstand damage caused by the hurricanes and typhoons which frequently occur in insular areas and such damage often costs millions of dollars to repair; and

the refinement of renewable energy technologies since the publication of the 1982 Territorial Energy Assessment prepared pursuant to subsection (c) reveals the need to reassess the state of energy production, consumption, infrastructure, reliance on imported energy, opportunities for energy conservation and increased energy efficiency, and indigenous sources in regard to the insular areas.

develop the renewable energy resources of the Caribbean and Pacific insular areas of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, the Marshall Islands, and Palau; and
to assist other insular areas in the Caribbean and Pacific Basin in the development of their renewable energy resources.
surveying existing sources and uses of energy;
estimating future energy needs to the year 2020, giving due consideration to a range of economic development possibilities;

assessing, in depth, the availability and potential for development of indigenous energy sources, including solar, wind, hydropower, ocean current and tidal, biogas, biofuel, geothermal and ocean thermal energy conversion;
assessing the mix of energy sources (including fossil fuels) and identifying those technologies that are needed to meet the projected demands for energy; and
drafting long-term energy plans for such insular areas with the objective of minimizing their reliance on energy imports and making maximum use of their indigenous energy resources.

children.heading: Demonstration of cost effective renewable energy technologies
children.label: d Demonstration of cost effective renewable energy technologies
children.text: The Secretary of Energy or any administrative official who may succeed him, with the approval of the Secretary of the Interior, as part of the comprehensive energy planning may demonstrate those indigenous renewable energy technologies which are determined to be most cost effective through the use of existing programs and may implement any projects or programs contained in recommendations of the plan.

children.heading: Updating of plans; submission to Congress
children.label: e Updating of plans; submission to Congress
A
children.text: updating the contents required by subsection (c);

children.label: B
children.text: drafting long-term energy plans for such insular areas with the objective of reducing, to the extent feasible, their reliance on energy imports by the year 2012, increasing energy conservation and energy efficiency, and maximizing, to the extent feasible, use of indigenous energy sources; and

children.label: C
children.text: drafting long-term energy transmission line plans for such insular areas with the objective that the maximum percentage feasible of electric power transmission and distribution lines in each insular area be protected from damage caused by hurricanes and typhoons.

children.label: A
children.text: improved supply-side efficiency of centralized electrical generation, transmission, and distribution systems;

children.label: i
children.text: the application of established standards for energy efficiency for appliances;

children.label: ii
children.text: the conduct of energy audits for business and industrial customers; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the use of energy savings performance contracts;

children.label: i
children.text: solar thermal energy for electric generation;

children.label: ii
children.text: solar thermal energy for water heating in large buildings, such as hotels, hospitals, government buildings, and residences;

children.label: iii
children.text: photovoltaic energy;

children.label: iv
children.text: wind energy;

children.label: v
children.text: hydroelectric energy;

children.label: vi
children.text: wave energy;

children.label: vii
children.text: energy from ocean thermal resources, including ocean thermal-cooling for community air conditioning;

children.label: viii
children.text: water vapor condensation for the production of potable water;

children.label: ix
children.text: fossil fuel and renewable hybrid electrical generation systems; and

children.label: x
children.text: other strategies or projects that the Secretary may identify as having significant potential; and

children.label: D
children.text: fuel substitution and minimization with indigenous biofuels, such as coconut oil.

In carrying out this subsection, for each insular area with a significant need for distributed generation, the Secretary of Energy shall identify and evaluate the most promising strategies and projects described in subparagraphs (C) and (D) of paragraph (2) for meeting that need.

children.label: i
children.text: any additional costs associated with the distribution of the generation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the long-term availability of the generation source;

children.label: B
children.text: the capacity of the local electrical utility to manage, operate, and maintain any project that may be undertaken; and

children.label: C
children.text: other factors the Secretary of Energy considers to be appropriate.

Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary of the Interior shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate, the Committee on Resources of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, the updated plans for each insular area required by this subsection.August 8, 2005

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are hereby authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.

The Secretary of Energy may grant financial assistance, not to exceed $2,000,000 annually, to insular area governments or private sector persons working in cooperation with insular area governments to carry out projects to evaluate the feasibility of, develop options for, and encourage the adoption of energy efficiency and renewable energy measures which reduce the dependency of the insular areas on imported fuels, improve the quality of the environment, and promote development in the insular areas.
Any applicant for financial assistance under this subsection must evidence coordination and cooperation with, and support from, the affected local energy institutions.

children.label: A
children.text: whether the measure will reduce the relative dependence of the insular area on imported fuels;

children.label: B
children.text: the ease and costs of operation and maintenance of any facilities contemplated as a part of the project;

children.label: C
children.text: whether the project will rely on the use of conservation measures or indigenous, renewable energy resources that were identified in the 1982 Territorial Energy Assessment or that are identified by the Secretary as consistent with the purposes of this subsection;

children.label: D
children.text: whether the measure will contribute significantly to development and the quality of the environment in the insular area; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other factors which the Secretary may determine to be relevant to a particular project.

children.heading: Power line grants for insular areas.—
children.label: 4 Power line grants for insular areas.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior is authorized to make grants to governments of insular areas of the United States to carry out eligible projects to protect electric power transmission and distribution lines in such insular areas from damage caused by hurricanes and typhoons.

children.label: i
children.text: The project is designed to protect electric power transmission and distribution lines located in 1 or more of the insular areas of the United States from damage caused by hurricanes and typhoons.

children.label: ii
children.text: The project is likely to substantially reduce the risk of future damage, hardship, loss, or suffering.

children.label: iii
children.text: The project addresses 1 or more problems that have been repetitive or that pose a significant risk to public health and safety.

children.label: iv
children.text: The project is not likely to cost more than the value of the reduction in direct damage and other negative impacts that the project is designed to prevent or mitigate. The cost benefit analysis required by this criterion shall be computed on a net present value basis.

children.label: v
children.text: The project design has taken into consideration long-term changes to the areas and persons it is designed to protect and has manageable future maintenance and modification requirements.

children.label: vi
children.text: The project plan includes an analysis of a range of options to address the problem it is designed to prevent or mitigate and a justification for the selection of the project in light of that analysis.

children.label: vii
children.text: The applicant has demonstrated to the Secretary that the matching funds required by subparagraph (D) are available.

children.label: i
children.text: have the greatest impact on reducing future disaster losses; and

children.label: ii
children.text: best conform with plans that have been approved by the Federal Government or the government of the insular area where the project is to be carried out for development or hazard mitigation for that insular area.

children.heading: .—Matching requirement
children.label: D .—Matching requirement
children.text: The Federal share of the cost for a project for which a grant is provided under this paragraph shall not exceed 75 percent of the total cost of that project. The non-Federal share of the cost may be provided in the form of cash or services.

children.heading: .—Treatment of funds for certain purposes
children.label: E .—Treatment of funds for certain purposes
children.text: Grants provided under this paragraph shall not be considered as income, a resource, or a duplicative program when determining eligibility or benefit levels for Federal major disaster and emergency assistance.

children.heading: .—Authorization of appropriations
children.label: F .—Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this paragraph $6,000,000 for each fiscal year beginning after .August 8, 2005

children.label: A
children.text: the term “insular area” means American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Federated States of Micronesia, Guam, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, the Republic of Palau, and the Virgin Islands; and

children.label: B
children.text: the term “1982 Territorial Energy Assessment” means the comprehensive energy plan prepared by the Secretary of Energy pursuant to subsection (c).
